items.title: Huawei maintains growth amid shifting demand and AI push
items.ai_impact: Huawei reported continued growth driven by shifting demand and investments in artificial intelligence (AI) technologies. Telecom infrastructure professionals can expect increased availability of AI-powered network management tools and potentially more competitive pricing for 5G and fiber deployment equipment from Huawei, impacting their technology selection and budgeting decisions.

items.title: Private 5G: Siemens goes global, Nokia still at the races, open RAN in the UK
items.ai_impact: Siemens has expanded its private 5G network offerings globally, while Nokia continues to compete in the market and open RAN technology is being tested in the UK. Telecom infrastructure professionals can expect increased demand for private 5G deployments and more opportunities to work with open RAN systems, requiring them to develop expertise in designing and deploying customized, secure, and scalable 5G networks for various industries.

items.title: Born in the USA – your next Wi-Fi router will be made in America (Analyst Angle)
items.ai_impact: The US government has initiated efforts to encourage Wi-Fi router manufacturing in America, aiming to reduce dependence on foreign suppliers. Telecom infrastructure professionals can expect increased availability of domestically produced Wi-Fi routers, potentially streamlining logistics and reducing lead times for fiber and 5G network deployments.
